stemHtml: On a climb, if you keep the thrust setting constant while maintaining a fixed vertical speed, what will happen to the aeroplane’s IAS?

options.a: It will increase, because the pilot will have to reduce pitch attitude to maintain vertical speed, which will tend to increase as TAS increases with altitude.
options.b: It will decrease, because the pilot will have to increase pitch attitude to maintain vertical speed as Excess Thrust decreases with altitude.
options.c: It will increase, because the rearward component of Lift decreases with altitude, and as the thrust setting remains constant, the aeroplane will accelerate.
options.d: It will decrease, because Drag increases with altitude, and as the trust settings remains constant, the aeroplane will decelerate.
